mismatched_fact_examples.truth_value: Proposed mechanism for nodule formation and internal oxidation at 600°C: Initially, Al2O3 nuclei cover part of the surface while FeO forms at random sites. CO/CO2 is produced from carbide decomposition at the interface and escapes through pores/microcracks, creating fast diffusion paths. Oxygen diffuses inward through FeO nodules, reacts with Al to form internal Al2O3 precipitates, depleting Al that would otherwise reach the interface and maintain a protective scale. This explains the non-simple parabolic kinetics.
mismatched_fact_examples.prediction_value: Table II parabolic rate constants summary: 600°C Kp1 = 2.4×10⁻¹² g² cm⁻⁴ sec⁻¹ (<4 h) and no Kp2; 700°C Kp1 = 6.4×10⁻¹¹ (<4 h), Kp2 = 2.2×10⁻¹² (>14 h); 800°C Kp1 = 4.9×10⁻¹² (<4 h), Kp2 = 0.6×10⁻¹² (>12 h); 900°C Kp1 = 5.6×10⁻¹² (<4 h), Kp2 = 1.0×10⁻¹³ (>10 h). Note: the main text states 2.4×10⁻¹⁰ for 600°C Kp1, which appears inconsistent with Table II and the kinetic trend in Fig. 2.
mismatched_fact_examples.truth_normalized: proposed mechanism for nodule formation and internal oxidation at 600 c : initially, al2o3 nuclei cover part of the surface while feo forms at random sites. co/co2 is produced from carbide decomposition at the interface and escapes through pores/microcracks, creating fast diffusion paths. oxygen diffuses inward through feo nodules, reacts with al to form internal al2o3 precipitates, depleting al that would otherwise reach the interface and maintain a protective scale. this explains the non-simple parabolic kinetics.
mismatched_fact_examples.prediction_normalized: table ii parabolic rate constants summary: 600 c kp1 = 2.4x10-12 g2 cm-4 sec-1 (<4 h) and no kp2; 700 c kp1 = 6.4x10-11 (<4 h), kp2 = 2.2x10-12 (>14 h); 800 c kp1 = 4.9x10-12 (<4 h), kp2 = 0.6x10-12 (>12 h); 900 c kp1 = 5.6x10-12 (<4 h), kp2 = 1.0x10-13 (>10 h). note: the main text states 2.4x10-10 for 600 c kp1, which appears inconsistent with table ii and the kinetic trend in fig. 2.
mismatched_fact_examples.reason: LLM judged same-field values as not semantically matched.

mismatched_fact_examples.truth_value: At 700°C and above, internal oxidation is suppressed by the formation of a healing Al2O3 or FeAl2O4 layer that blocks outward Fe cation diffusion. At 900°C, the Al flux is high enough to sustain a continuous Al2O3 scale, eliminating nodules entirely.
mismatched_fact_examples.prediction_value: Thermogravimetric data extracted from Fig. 2 (values read from table): at 700°C weight gain reached ~1.5 (arb. units) by 20 h and plateaued; at 600°C weight gain increased monotonically to ~1.3 at 30 h; at 800°C and 900°C weight gains were lower, reaching ~0.7 and ~0.4 respectively at 30 h. Exact y-axis units not stated in text.
mismatched_fact_examples.truth_normalized: at 700 c and above, internal oxidation is suppressed by the formation of a healing al2o3 or feal2o4 layer that blocks outward fe cation diffusion. at 900 c , the al flux is high enough to sustain a continuous al2o3 scale, eliminating nodules entirely.
